description: A man lies prone on rocky ground beneath a large gnarled tree, reaching eagerly into a pile of stones and earth. He wears a blue tunic with a yellow sash and leather sandals. A wooden staff or tool rests on the grass beside him. His expression conveys intense focus and excitement as he uncovers something hidden among the rocks. The setting is an open field with scattered stones and patches of green grass.
theological_description: This image depicts the Parable of the Hidden Treasure from Matthew 13:44, in which Jesus teaches that the kingdom of heaven is like treasure hidden in a field. When a man finds it, he hides it again and then, in his joy, sells everything he has to buy that field. The man's posture -- lying flat on the ground, reaching desperately into the earth -- conveys the overwhelming joy and urgency of discovering the incomparable value of the kingdom. The parable emphasizes that the kingdom of God is worth total sacrifice; nothing one possesses compares to its worth. Tissot renders the scene with striking realism, grounding the parable in the everyday world of a Palestinian laborer, yet the man's fervent body language elevates the moment beyond mere physical discovery to spiritual revelation. The hidden nature of the treasure also speaks to the mystery of the kingdom: it is present in the world but concealed from those who do not seek it.

_meta.title: The Hidden Treasure, illustration from 'The Life of Our Lord Jesus Christ'
